food_subgroup: Italian arborio rice dish with mushrooms, parmesan, white wine, chicken stock, and butter. 1 serve = ~350g.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for mushroom risotto (risotto ai funghi). Closest parent is the general risotto concept. No specific FoodOn term for this composite dish as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Rice dish — cooked rice-based composite dish (closest FoodEx2 parent for risotto)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for mushroom risotto. A00LC (rice dishes) used as closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for mushroom risotto as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of mushroom risotto is approximately 350g, equivalent to a generous bowl as typically served in a Western café or Italian restaurant in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Mushroom Risotto — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy composite (arborio rice, mushrooms, parmesan, butter, white wine, chicken stock)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for arborio rice (cooked), button mushrooms, shiitake mushrooms, parmesan cheese, unsalted butter, dry white wine, and chicken stock. Values represent a composite estimate for a typical mushroom risotto recipe at approximately 350g per serve. No single USDA entry exists for mushroom risotto as a composite dish; values are consistent with the input record which was verified against USDA SR Legacy component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; Arborio rice GI reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Arborio rice (short-grain, high-amylopectin) has a published GI of approximately 69 (medium) in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature, slightly lower than regular white rice (~72) due to its different starch structure and progressive starch release during slow cooking. GI of 69 applied to the composite mushroom risotto dish. GL per 350g serving = (69 × 21g carbohydrate per 100g × 3.5) / 100 = 50.8 (high GL). Fat and protein from butter and parmesan provide modest gastric emptying delay.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium ~543mg per 350g serving from parmesan and chicken stock
hypertension.guidance: Mushroom risotto provides approximately 543mg sodium per standard 350g serving, representing about 24% of the 2,300mg daily limit; individuals with hypertension should request low-sodium stock and limit parmesan quantity, or choose a smaller portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~50.8 per 350g serving) from refined arborio rice starch
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high glycemic load of approximately 50.8 per standard serving makes mushroom risotto unsuitable as a regular meal choice for people with Type 2 Diabetes; portion should be reduced to 150–200g, paired with a green salad and additional mushrooms to increase fibre and slow glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat ~7.7g per 350g serving from butter and parmesan; moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mushroom risotto contains approximately 7.7g saturated fat per serving from butter and parmesan, approaching one-third of the recommended daily limit of 20g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request reduced butter, and note that shiitake mushroom beta-glucan may provide a modest LDL-lowering benefit.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Shiitake mushroom beta-glucan (lentinan) with documented immune-modulating properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Shiitake mushrooms in this dish contain lentinan, a beta-glucan with anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties supported by animal and small human studies, making mushroom risotto a reasonable occasional choice for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis when portion size is managed.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mushroom Risotto (risotto ai funghi) is a classic northern Italian rice dish widely served in Western cafés and Italian restaurants in Singapore. It is made by slowly cooking short-grain arborio rice with sautéed button and shiitake mushrooms, dry white wine, chicken stock, butter, and parmesan cheese, stirring progressively to release the rice's natural starch and create a characteristically creamy, deeply savoury texture without added cream. The dish is rich and umami-forward, with earthy mushroom flavour balanced by the sharpness of parmesan and the acidity of white wine. Per 100g it provides 143 kcal, 3.5g protein, 5g fat (2.2g saturated), and 21g carbohydrate. A standard 350g café serving delivers approximately 500 kcal, 12g protein, 17.5g fat, and 73.5g carbohydrate with a high glycemic load of ~50.8. The arborio rice has a GI of approximately 69 (medium), slightly lower than regular white rice due to its high amylopectin content and slow-cooked starch release. Shiitake mushrooms contribute lentinan (beta-glucan), which has documented immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory properties relevant to rheumatoid arthritis management. The dish is halal and gluten-free. For Type 2 Diabetes management, the high GL requires strict portion control. For cardiovascular health, saturated fat from butter and parmesan should be noted. Sodium is moderate at ~543mg per serving. Mushroom risotto is a popular dinner option in Singapore's Western café dining scene.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from USDA SR Legacy component data for individual ingredients (arborio rice, mushrooms, parmesan, butter, white wine, chicken stock) assembled as a composite estimate. No single USDA or HPB entry exists for mushroom risotto as a complete dish. The GI of 69 is based on published arborio rice GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature, applied as an analogous estimate to the composite dish. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved-source entry covers the exact composite recipe.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite mushroom risotto dish is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB database; individual component fatty acid data could be used for future refinement. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite cooked dish is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy composite used. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed negligible/trace per USDA SR Legacy data for the component ingredients). All core macros (protein_g 3.5, fat_g 5.0, carbohydrate_g 21.0) are non-zero and supported by USDA SR Legacy component data. GL per serving calculated as: (GI 69 × carbohydrate_g per 100g 21.0 × serving_g 350) / 10000 = 50.8. Halal status based on ingredients (no pork, no alcohol used as a final ingredient — white wine is cooked off during preparation; halal certification of individual stock and cheese products should be verified at point of purchase). Gluten-free status based on arborio rice as the primary starch; cross-contamination risk exists in restaurant settings.
